name: MSSQL Add Account To Sysadmin Role
notes:
Converted from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
SIGMA Title: MSSQL Add Account To Sysadmin Role
SIGMA Description: Detects when an attacker tries to backdoor the MSSQL server by adding a backdoor account to the sysadmin fixed server role

False positives:
- Rare legitimate administrative activity

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event matches devicetype=12 AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%mssql%' AND "Event ID"=33205 AND (LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%object_name:sysadmin%' OR LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%statement:alter server role [sysadmin] add member %') AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY MSSQL Add Account To Sysadmin Role on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when the event matches devicetype=12 AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%mssql%' AND "Event ID"=33205 AND (LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%object_name:sysadmin%' OR LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%statement:alter server role [sysadmin] add member %') AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: MSSQL Add Account To Sysadmin Role
